HSN Code Invoicing

4-Digit vs 6-Digit HSN Codes: What is the Rule for 2026?

Understand the CBIC rules regarding 4-digit and 6-digit HSN codes on GST invoices based on your turnover limit.

18 June 2026

Entering the correct Harmonized System of Nomenclature (HSN) code on a tax invoice is a legal requirement under the GST regime. However, the level of detail required—specifically the number of digits—has been a source of confusion for many business owners.

If you generate B2B or B2C invoices, you must understand the rules regarding 4-digit versus 6-digit codes. You can search for the exact format required for your products using our free HSN Code Lookup tool.

The ₹5 Crore Turnover Rule

The CBIC (Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs) has simplified the requirement based on a business’s Aggregate Annual Turnover (AATO) in the preceding financial year.

1. Turnover up to ₹5 Crore

If your AATO is ₹5 Crore or less, the rules are slightly relaxed to reduce the compliance burden on MSMEs.

  • B2B Invoices: You MUST mention a 4-digit HSN Code.
  • B2C Invoices: Mentioning the HSN code is Optional (though it is highly recommended as a best practice).

2. Turnover Above ₹5 Crore

If your business is larger and exceeds the ₹5 Crore threshold, the government requires precise classification for data analytics and targeted policymaking.

  • All Invoices (B2B and B2C): You MUST mention a 6-digit HSN Code.

What About Exports?

The rules are different the moment your goods cross international borders. For all Imports and Exports, an 8-digit HSN Code is mandatory on all shipping bills and bills of entry, regardless of your turnover. This is required for customs clearance and claiming export benefits.

The Impact on GSTR-1

When you file your GSTR-1 return (track deadlines on our Due Dates Calendar), you must provide an HSN-wise summary of outward supplies (Table 12). If you fall in the >₹5 Crore bracket but only report 4-digit codes in Table 12, the GST portal will flag an error, and you may be blocked from filing, potentially triggering fees calculated by our Late Fee Calculator.

How to Find 4, 6, or 8-Digit Codes

Instead of pouring over massive PDF files released by the CBIC, use our HSN Code Lookup tool. It is designed to let you search broadly by name (e.g., “coffee”) and drill down from the 2-digit chapter, to the 4-digit heading, all the way down to the 6 and 8-digit tariff items.

(Pro Tip: Once you have the code, you can use our GST Calculator to ensure you are applying the correct 5%, 12%, 18%, or 28% tax slab before issuing the invoice).
